assumptions: `CLAR-001` locked: object storage only for uploads; DB base64 path excluded.
`CLAR-002` locked: phase-2 stays mock-auth only; real OIDC/Vendor MFA cutover deferred.
`CLAR-008` locked: staged capacity target for phase-2; no hard 25k RPS gate now.
Stage-1 domain behavior/OpenAPI baseline remain valid unless explicitly extended.
Monorepo remains Cargo workspace + `frontend/`; frontend calls backend only via generated TS client.

acceptanceSignals: One-command local startup works (`make dev*`).
Seed data covers lifecycle/compliance/dispute/anomaly/mapping scenarios.
executionConstraints: Compose stacks include core stateful dependencies.
`.env.development` is committed and `.env.local` is gitignored.
Makefile + `scripts/setup-dev.sh` are canonical entrypoints.
features.name: Local Dev Stack and Seed Baseline
features.rationale: Deterministic local startup is required for fast and reliable execution.
features.summary: Establish one-command local environment and realistic seed coverage.

acceptanceSignals: Migration up/down passes on clean PostgreSQL.
Schema invariants for money/enum/append-only are enforced.
executionConstraints: Use PostgreSQL 16+ with `sqlx migrate` only.
Use one global PK standard after `ISS-006` decision.
Use UTC timestamps + BIGINT minor units.
features.name: PostgreSQL Schema and Migration Foundation
features.rationale: Durable data correctness is prerequisite for every portal flow.
features.summary: Build audited relational schema and migration lifecycle.

acceptanceSignals: Domain flows run on real PostgreSQL/testcontainers.
`sqlx` compile-time checks pass.
In-memory production persistence paths are removed.
excludedApproaches: Mock-only DB verification for critical flows.
executionConstraints: Implement explicit SQL repos and transactions.
Tune `sqlx::PgPool` for operational limits.
Remove legacy in-memory production wiring.
features.name: Rust Persistence Integration
features.rationale: Backend behavior must be validated on durable state before UI rollout.
features.summary: Bind domain services to SQL repositories and transactions.

acceptanceSignals: Missing employee/vendor endpoints are delivered with auth.
OpenAPI + generated TS client regenerate and pass checks.
Pagination/filter/RBAC tests pass for added endpoints.
executionConstraints: Update handlers/spec/client atomically.
Keep semantics aligned with state machine + RBAC.
features.name: API Gap Closure and Contract Sync
features.rationale: Frontend implementation requires complete and stable contract surface.
features.summary: Close API flow gaps and enforce contract lockstep.

acceptanceSignals: SvelteKit hooks inject actor context and enforce role guards.
Expiry/refresh behaviors are handled safely.
Mock auth works for employee/vendor/admin in dev.
executionConstraints: Respect `CLAR-002` mock-auth-only scope.
Keep provider seam swappable for later real auth.
Centralize authn/authz in hooks/middleware.
features.name: Authentication Middleware (Mock-Only Phase Scope)
features.rationale: Role-safe access boundaries are required before portal features.
features.summary: Implement provider-agnostic auth architecture with mock runtime.

acceptanceSignals: Single app serves `/employee/*`, `/vendor/*`, `/admin/*` with role-aware nav.
zh-TW strings are centralized.
Global loading/offline/localized-error UX exists.
executionConstraints: Use SvelteKit 2.x + Tailwind 4.x + TypeScript.
Generated TS client is sole API access layer.
Maintain mobile-first employee UX; desktop-first vendor/admin UX with tablet stability.
features.name: Shared Frontend Platform and UX Baseline
features.rationale: Shared frontend primitives avoid cross-portal rework.
features.summary: Build common routing, i18n, async-state, and error-handling foundations.

acceptanceSignals: Menu/compliance upload + retrieval works via S3-compatible storage.
Invalid MIME/size is rejected with localized errors.
DB stores object references only.
executionConstraints: Respect `CLAR-001` object-storage-only policy.
Use presigned upload + metadata + thumbnails.
Enforce MIME allowlist and size limits per artifact class.
features.name: Object Storage Upload Pipeline
features.rationale: Vendor/admin workflows depend on reliable binary asset handling.
features.summary: Integrate object storage for images, compliance docs, and export artifacts.

acceptanceSignals: Valkey key/TTL/invalidation behavior is observable.
JetStream consumers are idempotent with retry + DLQ.
Concurrent stock decrement tests prevent oversell.
executionConstraints: Implement required keys/TTLs + write-through invalidation.
Configure `max_ack_pending` and dead-letter routing.
Keep DB authoritative for correctness-critical transitions.
features.name: Cache and Event Backbone (Valkey + JetStream)
features.rationale: Async/event correctness should exist before portal workflows consume it.
features.summary: Establish cache/event backbone with correctness safeguards.

acceptanceSignals: Employee flow works end-to-end (browse/order/modify/list/pickup).
Payroll summary/dispute submission/status tracking works.
Plant scope + role guards hold across UI/API.
executionConstraints: Implement calendar/week menus, stock-aware ordering, and cutoff countdown.
Render 30-second pickup QR refresh with mobile readability.
Keep in-app notifications core; channel expansion optional.
features.name: Employee Portal MVP
features.rationale: Employee ordering/pickup is the primary business value path.
features.summary: Deliver employee ordering and payroll-visibility portal.

acceptanceSignals: Vendor menu/status/order-window management works.
Fulfillment board/export batches/delivery updates work.
Vendor docs and operations filters are functional.
excludedApproaches: Exposing admin-only operations to vendor routes.
executionConstraints: Use status PATCH for 上架/暫停/下架.
Support printable exports with traceable batch IDs.
Maintain desktop-first UX with tablet-safe behavior.
features.name: Vendor Portal MVP
features.rationale: Supply-side execution is required for end-to-end operations.
features.summary: Deliver vendor menu, fulfillment, delivery, and ops workflows.

acceptanceSignals: Admin review/document lifecycle + plant mapping works.
Settlement lock/unlock/exception/dispute workflows work.
Anomaly/audit/analytics views support required filters.
excludedApproaches: Settlement export without fixed v1 contract.
executionConstraints: Capture review/suspension reasons and expiry handling.
Apply `ISS-003` before settlement release sign-off.
Apply `ISS-007` before anomaly release sign-off.
features.name: Committee Admin Portal MVP
features.rationale: Governance/compliance/settlement controls are required admin scope.
features.summary: Deliver admin compliance, settlement, anomaly, audit, and analytics workflows.

acceptanceSignals: PostgreSQL topology + PgBouncer RW/RO services are declared/testable.
Runtime uses pooled RW/RO endpoints (no direct DB pod connections).
Gateway/default-deny/ExternalSecret controls are active.
SvelteKit adapter-node frontend Deployment/Service + cache-control behavior are declared.
executionConstraints: Use PgBouncer transaction mode with separate RW/RO services.
Enforce TLS/CORS/request-size/rate-limit policies.
Implement secret-rotation-safe wiring (dual-key where required).
Include frontend runtime deployment contract from §12.8.
features.name: Runtime Infrastructure Security and Access Baseline
features.rationale: Runtime topology/security/deployment controls must be explicit to avoid late infra redesign.
features.summary: Deliver DB pool topology, frontend runtime deployment, edge controls, network isolation, and externalized secrets.

acceptanceSignals: `kustomize build` passes for dev/staging/production overlays.
HPA/KEDA + topology spread constraints exist for required components.
Overlay values consistently encode selected runtime strategy.
excludedApproaches: Single flat manifests for all environments.
executionConstraints: Maintain `base/components/overlays` structure.
Implement autoscaling + multi-AZ constraints per requirement.
Keep dev overlay lightweight but structurally aligned with staging/prod.
features.name: Kustomize Overlays, Multi-AZ, and Autoscaling
features.rationale: Environment promotion and scaling behavior must be codified before release automation finalization.
features.summary: Codify overlays, topology constraints, and autoscaling policy.

acceptanceSignals: Image-build/migration-check/e2e-smoke/deploy/load-gate workflows exist and pass.
Migration CI validates reversible up/down.
Production deploy consumes production overlay artifacts and emits evidence.
excludedApproaches: Manual production deploy without gate evidence.
executionConstraints: Add build/migration/smoke/deploy/load workflows.
Keep gates contract-aware + migration-aware.
Smoke coverage must include employee/vendor/admin critical paths.
features.name: CI/CD Expansion and Release Gates
features.rationale: Release safety controls should be finalized against complete portal and runtime baseline.
features.summary: Finalize CI/CD gates for build correctness, deploy safety, and release evidence.

acceptanceSignals: Staging load profiles meet staged phase targets.
Autoscaling converges under burst/decay without severe oscillation.
Tuning changes are committed as declarative config + benchmark evidence.
excludedApproaches: Treating 25k RPS as mandatory phase-2 gate.
executionConstraints: Apply staged ramp policy from `CLAR-008`.
Validate cache/queue/DB split behavior under load.
Use results as release-readiness evidence.
features.name: Staged Capacity Validation and Tuning
features.rationale: Capacity assertions require measured evidence under locked staged gate policy.
features.summary: Run staged load validation and tune scale parameters.

acceptanceSignals: Email/web-push channels can be enabled independently.
Dispatcher supports per-channel retry/observability/failure isolation.
Channel toggles are controlled by feature flags + preferences.
excludedApproaches: Coupling channel expansion to MVP core release.
executionConstraints: Deliver channel expansion behind feature flags.
Keep in-app notifications as baseline core path.
Maintain idempotent per-channel handling.
features.name: Optional Notification Channel Expansion
features.rationale: Additional channels can coexist and should not block MVP completion.
features.summary: Add email/web-push notifications as optional feature-flag scope.

options.label: CSV only (v1)
options.rationale: Fastest and most auditable MVP contract for HR sync.

options.label: XLSX only (v1)
options.rationale: Useful when HR requires strict native Excel formatting immediately.

options.label: CSV + XLSX both (v1)
options.rationale: Higher implementation and test scope for phase-2.

clarifications.prompt: 月結扣款匯出 v1 要交付哪一種格式？
clarifications.rationale: Settlement acceptance cannot be finalized without one v1 contract.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Lock v1 to CSV and add XLSX later only if necessary.

issues.details: Requirement allows CSV/Excel, but v1 export contract is not locked.
issues.title: Payroll Export v1 Format Not Locked

options.label: In-app notifications
options.rationale: Lowest complexity and sufficient baseline for MVP.

options.label: Email notifications
options.rationale: Adds out-of-app reach for key alerts.

options.label: Web push notifications
options.rationale: Real-time channel with additional permission/ops overhead.

clarifications.prompt: MVP 與後續階段要啟用哪些通知通道？
clarifications.rationale: Channel selection impacts dispatcher design and ops load.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Keep in-app core; deliver email/web-push as optional flagged scope.

issues.details: Notification behavior is required while channel combination is intentionally open.
issues.title: Notification Channel Scope Selection

options.label: Hybrid (dev self-host, prod managed when available)
options.rationale: Balances IaC ownership with operational overhead.

options.label: Self-host everywhere
options.rationale: Highest control with highest operations burden.

options.label: Managed everywhere possible
options.rationale: Lower ops burden with less in-repo runtime ownership.

clarifications.prompt: 正式環境基礎設施主策略要採哪一種？
clarifications.rationale: Strategy choice materially changes overlay and manifest ownership.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Lock one primary strategy now to avoid infra rework.

issues.details: Multiple runtime models are allowed, but one project strategy remains unlocked.
issues.title: Infrastructure Runtime Strategy Not Fixed

options.label: UUIDv7
options.rationale: Best fit for ordered indexing and audit trace patterns.

clarifications.prompt: 主鍵標準要統一採 UUIDv7 還是 ULID？
clarifications.rationale: Mixed PK standards increase schema and API complexity.

issues.details: Schema permits UUIDv7 or ULID, but project-wide standard is not locked.
issues.title: Global Primary Key Standard Ambiguity

clarifications.prompt: 異常告警 SLA 的計算政策要採哪個模式？
clarifications.rationale: Anomaly dashboard requires deterministic SLA computation for auditability.
clarifications.recommendationSummary: Adopt severity defaults with optional per-rule overrides.

issues.details: SLA states are defined, but threshold computation policy is not locked.
issues.title: Anomaly SLA Computation Policy Undefined

activation.rationale: Contract drift breaks all portals and CI guarantees.

checks: API change PRs include OpenAPI spec updates.
Generated TS client artifacts are regenerated/committed with contract changes.
Contract verification CI passes before merge.

reinforcements.instructions: Treat backend handlers, OpenAPI, and generated client as one atomic interface boundary.
reinforcements.name: Contract Lockstep Gate
reinforcements.policy: No API change ships without synchronized contract artifacts.

activation.rationale: Data-layer defects are expensive and hard to reverse.

checks: Migration up/down passes on clean PostgreSQL.
Critical transaction tests run on real PostgreSQL/testcontainers.
Schema checks confirm BIGINT money, UTC timestamps, append-only event tables.

reinforcements.instructions: Reject data-layer changes lacking migration proof and real-DB verification.
reinforcements.name: Persistence Integrity Gate
reinforcements.policy: Durable data behavior must be migration-driven and real-DB tested.

activation.rationale: Role/scope leakage is a direct security defect.

checks: Route guards enforce employee/vendor/admin isolation.
Token expiry/refresh success and rejection paths are covered.
Privileged API calls fail with invalid role/scope claims.

reinforcements.instructions: Centralize and verify auth boundaries across UI and API.
reinforcements.name: Authorization Boundary Gate
reinforcements.policy: Identity, role, and scope checks must be consistent end-to-end.

activation.rationale: File handling impacts security, compliance, and cost.

checks: Uploads validate MIME/size before persistence.
No runtime DB base64 storage path remains.
Bucket access policies match required public/private boundaries.

reinforcements.instructions: Block any reintroduction of excluded file-storage paths.
reinforcements.name: Canonical File Storage Gate
reinforcements.policy: All binary artifacts must use validated object-storage flow.

activation.rationale: Async/cache regressions can silently corrupt business behavior across ordering and fulfillment flows.

checks: Cache invalidation proves read-after-write consistency.
Consumers remain idempotent under replay/retry.
DLQ routing and alerting trigger after retry threshold.

reinforcements.instructions: Keep DB authoritative and event consumers idempotent.
reinforcements.name: Cache and Event Correctness Gate
reinforcements.policy: Performance layers must not violate business correctness.

activation.rationale: UX reliability defects block operational adoption.

checks: Async actions show loading and suppress duplicate submits.
Offline/API errors render understandable zh-TW messages.
Responsive checks pass for employee mobile and vendor/admin desktop-tablet.

reinforcements.instructions: Fail review when loading/error/offline/responsive behavior is incomplete.
reinforcements.name: UX Reliability Gate
reinforcements.policy: User-facing flows must remain resilient, localized, and device-appropriate.

activation.rationale: Runtime security baseline must be in place before promotion.

checks: Gateway enforces TLS, CORS, body-size, and rate-limit policies.
NetworkPolicy default-deny + allowlist matrix is validated.
ExternalSecret-sourced credentials are used; plaintext secrets are absent.

reinforcements.instructions: Treat edge/network/secret posture as hard release criteria.
reinforcements.name: Runtime Security Gate
reinforcements.policy: Production runtime must implement least privilege and externalized secret management.

activation.rationale: Release controls need explicit ownership separate from runtime controls.

checks: Workflow files exist for build, migration-check, smoke, deploy, and load-gate.
Required workflows are wired to expected branch/PR triggers.
Failure in release gates blocks promotion.

reinforcements.instructions: Keep release safety checks deterministic and mandatory.
reinforcements.name: Release Pipeline Integrity Gate
reinforcements.policy: Promotion is allowed only through passing automated release gates.

activation.rationale: Scale readiness must be demonstrated with repeatable evidence.

checks: Staged load profiles and SLO outcomes are published as CI artifacts.
Autoscaling behavior is measured for burst and decay phases.
Tuning updates are committed as declarative config changes.

reinforcements.instructions: Use staged benchmark evidence to tune and justify readiness.
reinforcements.name: Scale Evidence Gate
reinforcements.policy: Capacity claims require reproducible benchmark evidence and config traceability.

activation.rationale: Project policy rejects legacy compatibility and unverified fallback paths.

checks: In-memory production persistence path is removed post SQL cutover.
Excluded options are not reintroduced (DB base64 storage, real-auth cutover in phase-2).
Optional capabilities stay behind explicit feature flags.

reinforcements.instructions: Continuously remove obsolete compatibility branches and keep one verified core path.
reinforcements.name: No-Legacy/Fallback Gate
reinforcements.policy: Core runtime must remain singular, clean, and verified.

roles.name: Employee
responsibilities: Browse plant-scoped menus and place/modify orders before cutoff.
Track order timeline and complete pickup verification.
Review payroll deductions/refunds and submit disputes.

roles.name: Vendor Operator
responsibilities: Manage menu items, availability, and ordering windows.
Operate fulfillment board, exports, and delivery status updates.
Upload compliance documents and monitor order/revenue views.

roles.name: Committee Admin
responsibilities: Review vendor compliance and document lifecycle decisions.
Manage vendor-plant mappings and settlement/dispute workflows.
Operate anomaly governance, audit lookup, and operations analytics.

roles.name: HR/Payroll Consumer
responsibilities: Consume payroll export outputs for deduction processing.
Report reconciliation mismatches to operations/admin.

roles.name: Backend Engineer
responsibilities: Implement SQLx persistence, domain transactions, and API handlers.
Maintain OpenAPI and generated client consistency.
Build queue consumers/workers with idempotency and retry/DLQ behavior.

roles.name: Frontend Engineer
responsibilities: Build SvelteKit employee/vendor/admin portals and shared architecture.
Integrate generated client, route guards, i18n, and async/error UX.
Deliver responsive behavior for role-specific device contexts.

roles.name: Platform/SRE Engineer
responsibilities: Own runtime manifests, overlays, autoscaling, and topology constraints.
Operate gateway, DB pool topology, cache, queue, storage, and frontend runtime deployments.
Maintain CI/CD deployment reliability and load-gate execution.

roles.name: Security/IAM Engineer
responsibilities: Enforce auth boundary patterns and token validation controls.
Define secret sourcing/rotation and key-window practices.
Review gateway/network isolation policies before release.

roles.name: QA/Automation Engineer
responsibilities: Implement integration/e2e/contract/load validation suites.
Validate critical end-to-end journeys across all portals.
Track regression risk and release evidence quality.

roles.name: Product/Delivery Lead
responsibilities: Lock unresolved clarifications and acceptance contracts.
Sequence backlog execution and dependency timing.
Own core-vs-optional release scope decisions.

summary: No additional semantic corrections are required. Feature boundaries, dependency graph, ordering, and reinforcement ownership are internally consistent and executable for phase-2 scope with current locked decisions.
